signals.name: Ambient AI Integration
signals.description: AI becoming invisible infrastructure embedded in everyday environments rather than discrete tools.

signals.name: Longevity Economy
signals.description: Consumer markets reshaping around extended healthy lifespans; 100-year planning horizons entering mainstream.

signals.name: Regenerative Business Models
signals.description: Companies redesigning operations to restore ecological and social systems rather than merely reduce harm.

signals.name: Spatial Computing Interfaces
signals.description: Mixed reality becoming a primary computing surface, replacing screen-based interaction for knowledge work.

signals.name: Trust Deficit in Institutions
signals.description: Accelerating erosion of public trust in governments, media, and corporations creating alternative authority structures.

signals.name: Synthetic Media Normalization
signals.description: AI-generated content indistinguishable from human-made becoming baseline for creative and informational media.

signals.name: Microbiome as Health Architecture
signals.description: Gut and skin microbiome interventions emerging as primary levers for disease prevention and mental health.

signals.name: Deglobalization Corridors
signals.description: Trade realigning along geopolitical blocs rather than efficiency, creating parallel supply chains.

signals.name: Phantom Work Economy
signals.description: Proliferation of AI agents doing knowledge work previously requiring human labor, blurring employment metrics.

signals.name: Liquid Identity
signals.description: Individuals maintaining multiple authentic identity layers across digital, physical, and synthetic contexts simultaneously.

signals.name: Circular Material Passports
signals.description: Digital tracking of material origin and recyclability becoming mandatory infrastructure for product design.

signals.name: Sovereign AI Infrastructure
signals.description: Nation-states building independent AI compute and data infrastructure as a strategic defense priority.

signals.name: Affective Computing
signals.description: Systems that detect and respond to human emotional states, embedding empathy as a design primitive.

signals.name: Climate Migration Finance
signals.description: Financial instruments emerging to fund mass population relocation driven by uninhabitable climate zones.

signals.name: Neuro-enhancement Mainstreaming
signals.description: Cognitive enhancement technologies moving from fringe medical use to consumer wellness and workplace productivity.

trends.name: Machine Intelligence Diffusion
trends.description: AI capabilities distributing across all sectors simultaneously, compressing the adoption curve that previous general-purpose technologies followed over decades.

trends.name: Human Redefinition
trends.description: Biological, psychological, and social definitions of humanity under simultaneous pressure from longevity science, AI cognition, and identity fluidity.

trends.name: Reality Fragmentation
trends.description: Shared reality dissolving as synthetic media, personalized feeds, and virtual environments make consensus experience structurally impossible.

trends.name: Systemic Sustainability Transition
trends.description: Environmental limits forcing redesign of industrial, agricultural, and urban systems at civilizational scale.

trends.name: Multipolar World Order
trends.description: Geopolitical power distributing away from post-Cold War unipolarity into competing blocs with incompatible governance, technology, and value systems.

scenarios.name: The Augmented Professional
scenarios.description: Knowledge workers operate as AI orchestrators rather than producers, with human value residing in judgment, ethics, and relationship. Organizations restructure around human-AI teams as the base unit.

scenarios.name: The Automation Fracture
scenarios.description: AI-driven productivity gains concentrate in capital, while displaced labor lacks retraining infrastructure. Social contract around work collapses in high-income economies first.

scenarios.name: Century Lives, Broken Systems
scenarios.description: Lifespans extending to 120+ while pension, healthcare, and retirement systems designed for 75-year horizons fail. New institutions emerge slowly; a lost generation funds both ends of the age curve.

scenarios.name: Epistemological Collapse
scenarios.description: Synthetic media saturation makes shared factual ground unachievable. Verification infrastructure races against generation infrastructure and loses. Governance, courts, and journalism require foundational redesign.

scenarios.name: The Consensus Brokers
scenarios.description: New institutions emerge as trusted arbiters of reality — certified media environments, cryptographic provenance systems, and deliberative assemblies that reconstruct shared ground from fragmented landscapes.

scenarios.name: The Regenerative Industrial Complex
scenarios.description: Carbon, biodiversity, and water markets mature into primary capital allocation mechanisms. Companies that cannot price externalities lose access to institutional capital. Industrial geography rewrites around resource renewal.

scenarios.name: Bloc Technology
scenarios.description: Incompatible AI, communications, and financial infrastructure splits the global digital economy into 2-3 sovereign stacks. Cross-bloc operations require active bridging; multinational business model collapses for mid-tier firms.

scenarios.name: Managed Multipolarity
scenarios.description: Major powers negotiate functional agreements on climate, AI safety, and pandemic response while competing on economics and influence. A minimal viable multilateralism holds enough global infrastructure together to avoid catastrophic fragmentation.
